non_blocking_findings.dimension: completeness / security negative-proof coverage (F5 second negative is doc-only, not test-asserted)
non_blocking_findings.location: crates/overdrive-dataplane/tests/integration/mtls_guardrails.rs:32-39 (AC5 docstring) vs the AC5 test body :391-412
non_blocking_findings.description: Roadmap criterion 5 names TWO F5 negatives: (a) the agent's peer-facing dial is NOT re-intercepted (no recursion); (b) a workload that sets the bypass on its OWN socket is STILL intercepted (agent-private). The test body asserts ONLY (a) -- a happy outbound enforce reaching Established proves the agent's leg-B dial was not re-intercepted (mtls_guardrails.rs:391-412). Negative (b) appears only in the AC5 docstring (:34-36); NO test assertion exercises it (orchestrator-verified: grep of the AC5 body finds the no-recursion assertion at :412 and no self-exempt assertion). This is NOT theater -- the exemption is STRUCTURAL: cgroup_connect4_mtls attaches to the WORKLOAD cgroup subtree, not the agent's, so there is no per-socket bypass knob a workload could set; the workload cannot move itself out of its own cgroup. Negative (b) is therefore structurally unrepresentable as a runtime assertion at the 04-01 adapter boundary (enforce is handed an already-accepted leg; the intercept-INSTALL that scopes the cgroup attach is explicitly 05-01 scope per roadmap 05-01 implementation_notes 'the F5 NEGATIVE proofs ... are 04-01 scope -- only the mechanism + the install land [in 05-01]'). The honest state: negative (a) is asserted; negative (b) is guaranteed by the cgroup-attach boundary (proven structurally at 01-01, f5_intercept_exemption PASS) and only becomes an end-to-end runtime negative once 05-01 wires the production install. The gap is the docstring claiming AC5 covers both negatives as if both were test-asserted.
non_blocking_findings.fix: Either (1) soften the AC5 docstring (:32-39) to state explicitly that negative (b) is structurally guaranteed by the workload-subtree cgroup attach boundary (not a runtime assertion at this tier) and is exercised end-to-end at 05-01 when the production intercept-install lands; OR (2) add the runtime self-exempt negative to 05-01's mtls_production_activation.rs (a workload attempting any bypass on its own socket is still routed through the agent). Recommend (1) for 04-01 (one-line doc honesty) + ensure 05-01 carries the runtime (b) negative. Non-blocking: the security property holds structurally; the finding is doc-claim-vs-test-coverage precision, the same 'don't call it proven until it is' discipline the #178 gating already honours.

non_blocking_findings.dimension: doc precision (AC2 'distinct reason' is distinct STRINGS within one variant, not distinct variants)
non_blocking_findings.description: Roadmap criterion 2 says inbound nocert/wrongca 'each reject with their DISTINCT reason'. The implementation maps BOTH to MtlsEnforcementError::PeerVerificationFailed { reason }, distinguished by the reason STRING ('peer sent no certificates' vs the untrusted-CA chain failure), and the test asserts assert_ne! on the two reason strings (:382-384) plus server_bytes==0 for both. On the security-critical slice where a divergent contract would be most dangerous, the implementation holds the #1 rule (CLAUDE.md 'implement to the design, never invent API surface') exactly: the MtlsEnforcement trait stays 4-method probe/enforce/liveness/teardown with enforce_outbound/inbound as PRIVATE delegates, every error variant + PumpLiveness::Stalled traces to a named requirement, no catch-all, expected_peer==None and PeerIdentityMismatch reserved-but-never-produced in v1 (the #178 gate honoured, nothing called 'protected'). Fail-closed is cause-distinct (AbsentSvid/AbsentBundle/PeerVerificationFailed distinct variants; nocert/wrongca distinct reason strings with assert_ne! + server 0 bytes). The F4/F7 limits trip at CONCRETE values (real 256 KiB+1 overflow -> BufferLimitExceeded; 128-real-concurrent-pre-arm 129th -> InFlightLimitExceeded) with mutation-grade boundary unit tests naming the </<=/>=/== mutant each kills (the RED_UNIT SKIPPED -> EXECUTED supersession is honest -- the tests exist, additive, no G9 violation). F6 supervision recomputes Stalled from inputs (persist-inputs rule), the worker tears down only Stalled -> Gone with no false positive and both telemetry events, production carries no sim-shaped contortion. The Tier-3 gate genuinely executes (panics-not-skips on unsupported, defeating the --no-run hole) and is correctly in the mtls-shared max-threads=1 nextest group (the 03-01 B1 remediation), and all three prior-review findings (01-01 forward-as-splice doc drift, 03-01 B1 group, 03-01 S1 path) were learned from. Two NON-blocking findings: Q1 -- the F5 SECOND negative (workload-cannot-self-exempt) is structurally guaranteed by the workload-subtree cgroup attach (no per-socket bypass knob exists) and proven structurally at 01-01, but is doc-claimed in the AC5 docstring as if test-asserted while only the no-recursion negative is asserted at 04-01 (the runtime self-exempt negative is only representable once 05-01 wires the production intercept-install); soften the docstring + ensure 05-01 carries the runtime (b) negative. Q2 -- 'distinct reason' for nocert/wrongca is satisfied at the reason-STRING level within one PeerVerificationFailed variant (defensible: rustls' distinction is not a stable typed surface), recorded so a future reader does not expect two variants. Neither blocks: both are doc-claim-vs-coverage precision, not a security or correctness defect. RECOMMENDATION: 04-01 is APPROVED to unblock 05-01; fold Q1 (docstring softening) into the next touch of mtls_guardrails.rs and ensure 05-01 carries the runtime self-exempt negative + the end-to-end external-validity gate.

blocking_issues.dimension: design-fidelity + deferral-discipline (false production doc + untracked shared-resource hazard)
blocking_issues.location: crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/mtls/dataplane.rs:189-208 (load); :192-194 (misleading comment); :196 (destructive discard). Cross-ref hash_of_maps.rs new_with_inner (fresh BPF_MAP_CREATE, no BPF_OBJ_GET); lib.rs:432-443 / :1451-1462 (EbpfDataplane pins+unlinks SERVICE_MAP at the SAME pin_dir); wave-decisions.md:1006-1013 (the reuse contract).
blocking_issues.description: MtlsDataplane::load does `remove_file(pin_dir/SERVICE_MAP)` then `new_pinned_with_array_inner(...)` -- which (verified by tracing new_with_array_inner -> new_with_inner) does a FRESH BPF_MAP_CREATE for both inner-prototype and outer-HoM, then bpf_obj_pin's the freshly-created outer fd. There is NO BPF_OBJ_GET reuse path anywhere in that constructor. This is the INVERSE of the design's load-bearing Load-model claim (wave-decisions.md:1006-1013: 'reuses the already-pinned outer FD via BPF_OBJ_GET rather than creating a second one ... never touches SERVICE_MAP through this surface'). The inline comment at dataplane.rs:192-194 ('so aya reuses the pinned outer FD') documents behaviour the code does not perform (development.md No-aspirational-docs violation). In this STANDALONE step the divergence is MASKED -- the AT uses a per-test isolated pin dir (/sys/fs/bpf/overdrive-mtls-dpoi-<pid>) where no EbpfDataplane ever pinned -- so all 06-01 ACs pass. But D-MTLS-17 item 3 wires load() into run_server at the PRODUCTION pin_dir /sys/fs/bpf/overdrive where EbpfDataplane has ALREADY pinned a live SERVICE_MAP the kernel LB service program is bound to by name. There, load()'s remove_file unlinks the live LB pin and new_pinned_with_array_inner creates a divergent EMPTY outer HoM pinned over it: any later by-name BPF_OBJ_GET (reconciler re-resolve, restart, operator bpftool) resolves the wrong map, and EbpfDataplane::Drop (lib.rs:1451-1462) removes a pin that now belongs to MtlsDataplane (silent cross-ownership). The hazard is NEITHER surfaced (the comment claims the opposite) NOR tracked (no issue, no // HAZARD/TODO(06-03) marker) -- violating CLAUDE.md 'implement to the design, never invent API surface' AND the deferral discipline ('deferred hazards must be explicitly surfaced and tracked').
blocking_issues.fix: Preferred: implement the design as written -- recover the EXISTING pin via bpf_obj_get(pin_path) + std::mem::forget; never remove_file+fresh-create on a path a production map may own. If aya 0.13.x makes BPF_OBJ_GET-reuse genuinely infeasible, the design's reuse claim is unsatisfiable and must be corrected via architect dispatch, AND load() must (a) replace `let _ = remove_file` at :196 with the discriminating NotFound-vs-real ErrorKind match EbpfDataplane::new already uses (lib.rs:437-443), and (b) carry an issue-tracked // HAZARD(06-03) marker naming the shared-pin cross-ownership risk + the required two-load sequencing, surfaced to the user for issue creation BEFORE 06-03 lands. Independent of the chosen path, fix the misleading :192-194 comment. NOTE: 06-01's own standalone ACs are met and the AT genuinely passes -- this blocks on the production-correctness / design-contract grounds and the untracked-hazard-into-06-03 grounds, not on a standalone-behaviour failure.

non_blocking_findings.dimension: mutation coverage gap (non-ENOENT branch + map_io_error discrimination unkilled)
non_blocking_findings.description: implementation_notes promise mutation >=80% on 'the program/unprogram branch'. The POD conversions ARE covered by default-lane proptests (:404-422). But program_redirect/unprogram_redirect need a loaded map (Tier-3). The Tier-3 AT exercises the happy remove (:135) and the ENOENT/idempotent-remove path (:141-143) but NEVER induces a non-ENOENT map error, so a mutation collapsing unprogram_redirect to always-Ok(()), or making map_io_error always return an ENOENT-shaped error, likely SURVIVES even on the Lima mutation path. Inducing a real EPERM/EFAULT map-delete against a live kernel map is genuinely awkward, hence high not blocking.
non_blocking_findings.fix: Extract the ENOENT-vs-real discrimination into a pure default-lane fn -- e.g. classify_remove(&io::Error) -> {Idempotent | Surface} -- and proptest it over raw_os_error in {ENOENT, EPERM, EFAULT, EINVAL, None}, asserting ENOENT->Idempotent and all else->Surface. Kills the == Some(ENOENT) flip and the always-Ok collapse on the LOGIC without needing a failing kernel syscall. Mirrors the supervision::derive_liveness extraction precedent.

non_blocking_findings.dimension: test strength (MISS assertion proves 'not rewritten', not 'passes through unchanged')
non_blocking_findings.description: AC wording is 'a connect to an un-programmed dest passes through unchanged (map MISS -> pass)'. The test asserts !lands_on_leg_f(unprogrammed) -- the connect does NOT arrive on leg-F within the 3s window. This proves 'not rewritten onto leg-F' (necessary) but NOT 'passes through unchanged' (sufficient): a kernel-side bug that DROPPED the connect on miss would produce the same observable (nothing on leg-F) and the test would still pass. The kernel miss-behaviour is owned by a prior step (the cgroup_connect4_mtls program already shipped) and the rewrite-fires POSITIVE (AC3a) carries the load, hence medium.
non_blocking_findings.fix: Strengthen the negative: point the 'un-programmed' dest at a SECOND reachable listener (a real sink on the host veth) and assert the connect lands THERE unchanged, not on leg-F. Converts 'absence on leg-F' into 'presence at the intended dest', which a drop-on-miss bug would fail. Not blocking (kernel miss-path is out of this step's production scope).

non_blocking_findings.dimension: POD layout lockstep defense adequate despite duplication (no Tier-2 backstop)
non_blocking_findings.description: userspace + kernel-side MtlsDestKey/MtlsAddrPort are byte-identical #[repr(C)] {ip_host:u32, port_host:u16, _pad:u16}, both with const _: () = assert!(size_of==8) guards, but DUPLICATED (no shared crate). Given cgroup_sock_addr has no BPF_PROG_TEST_RUN (no Tier-2), the defense is genuinely three-layered and sufficient: (1) size_of==8 compile guards on BOTH sides catch width/field-count drift; (2) default-lane proptests (:404-422) pin the exact 8-byte host-order wire layout (ip.to_ne ++ port.to_ne ++ 0u16) -- kills endianness/transpose/non-zero-pad mutations; (3) the Tier-3 map_has_redirect_entry oracle dumps the REAL kernel map via bpftool and matches the host-order key/value bytes -- a byte oracle against the actual kernel, which catches even a field-order swap that keeps size_of==8 true. Optional future hardening (NOT required for this step): a shared no_std-compatible #[repr(C)] POD would make drift unrepresentable rather than caught -- worth a follow-up only if a third consumer appears.

Root cause of the false-green: the failing hygiene block (test :281-312, the leak + assert-exactly-1) was added in the LATER mutation-gap-closure commit 93ca1eab (2026-06-14T13:50Z), AFTER the execution-log GREEN/COMMIT phases were logged (13:22-13:26Z, against the feat commit 4bfa49e6 whose test had no such assertion). blocking_issues.dimension: concurrent-resource safety (global shared kernel state) -- forward landmine for 06-03 per-alloc lifecycle; SAME defect class as 06-01 D1
blocking_issues.location: crates/overdrive-worker/src/mtls_intercept.rs:48-59 (NFT_TABLE='overdrive-mtls', TPROXY_FWMARK=0x1, TPROXY_RT_TABLE=100 -- all fixed constants), :205-262 (install_inbound_tproxy), :210 + :425-448 (unconditional preclean_global_inbound_state). Contrast the reference harness which keys the table per-context: mtls_netns_topology.rs:362 (overdrive_mtls_ws_{tag}).
blocking_issues.description: install_inbound_tproxy(virt, agent_port) is a PER-ALLOCATION free function (06-03 drives it on the per-alloc lifecycle), but the nft table name, fwmark, and routing-table number are all FIXED global constants, and the function OPENS with preclean_global_inbound_state() which unconditionally deletes table ip overdrive-mtls and drains the fwmark rules. Two adversarial verifiers CONFIRMED: a second server allocation's install precleans away the first allocation's live inbound intercept (table + ip rule), silently breaking the first server's mTLS interception. The function as written supports EXACTLY ONE concurrent inbound intercept per node. This is masked in 06-02 because the AT installs only one. It is the SAME structural defect class as 06-01's blocking D1 (a global shared kernel resource whose standalone test masks the collision via isolation, becoming a composition landmine for the next step) -- a recurring pattern in this production-integration phase that adversarial review exists to catch before 06-03 builds on it.
blocking_issues.fix: Resolve before 06-03 wires per-alloc inbound. EITHER (a) confirm + DOCUMENT that single-concurrent-inbound-intercept is the v1 scope (inbound server-resolution is #178-deferred; AC5 is OUTBOUND-proven) with a tracked // HAZARD(06-03) marker naming the constraint, OR (b) key the table name (and scope the preclean) per-virt WITHIN the pinned signature -- the table can derive from `virt` with NO signature change, mirroring the harness's per-context overdrive_mtls_ws_{tag}; the unconditional 'delete the whole table' preclean must become a scope-to-this-virt removal so it cannot destroy a sibling's intercept. This touches the pinned D-MTLS-14 surface's BEHAVIOUR, not its signature -- surface as a design question to the user/architect; do NOT let a crafter invent a signature change.

non_blocking_findings.dimension: test coverage gap on a security-relevant line (F5 leg-S exemption) -- CONFIRMED by adversarial verifier
non_blocking_findings.location: crates/overdrive-worker/src/mtls_intercept.rs:241-247 (the `meta mark {leg_s_mark} accept` exemption, ordered before the tproxy rule) vs tests/integration/mtls_intercept_install.rs:316-329 (nft-dump assertions).
non_blocking_findings.description: The production nft prerouting program ships the F5 leg-S no-recursion exemption (`meta mark <MTLS_LEG_S_DIAL_MARK> accept` first), which prevents the agent's own leg-S dial from being TPROXY'd back onto leg C (an infinite-recursion / bypass hazard). The test asserts the dump contains the tproxy line and the virt daddr/dport, but NEVER asserts the exemption line is present, and there is no recursion scenario (no agent-marked dial that should bypass the redirect). A grep for `meta mark.*accept` in the test file returns zero matches. The no-recursion BEHAVIOUR is proven at 01-01 (composed walking skeleton) and the line byte-matches the proven harness's production half, hence HIGH not blocking -- but a security-relevant line ships with no assertion in the step that introduces it.
non_blocking_findings.fix: When repairing the test (D1), add `assert!(table_dump.contains(&format!("meta mark {} accept", MTLS_LEG_S_DIAL_MARK)))` and assert it appears BEFORE the tproxy line; optionally add a scenario that dials with the MTLS_LEG_S_DIAL_MARK SO_MARK set and asserts it is NOT redirected to leg C.

non_blocking_findings.dimension: robustness -- fragile preclean drain loop (mechanism behind D1; root cause not fully isolated)
non_blocking_findings.description: preclean's drain loop is `for _ in 0..64 { if !matches!(run_best_effort(del), Ok(s) if s.success()) { break; } }`. It BREAKS on the first `ip rule del` whose `.status()` is not Ok(success). Orchestrator investigation: calling the production install_inbound_tproxy directly drains correctly (install=1) in some build configs but drains 0 (install=3) under the gated nextest/example build -- deterministically. A raw `.output()`-based del and an inline copy of run_best_effort BOTH drain correctly in the gated build, and the dependency tree is byte-identical with/without the feature (no reaper crate pulled in), so the exact root cause of the in-process `.status()` non-success under the gated build is NOT yet isolated and warrants a focused debugging pass (per .claude/rules/debugging.md). Regardless of root cause, the break-on-first-non-success drain loop is the fragile surface: a single spurious/transient non-success silently leaves stale rules.
non_blocking_findings.fix: Harden the drain: do NOT break the loop on the first non-success -- either iterate the full bounded count regardless, or use `.output()` (which drained reliably in repro) instead of `.status()`+null stdio, or distinguish the legitimate 'no such rule' stop (RTNETLINK ENOENT/exit 2) from a spurious wait error. AND complete the root-cause investigation of why the in-process `.status()` reports non-success under the gated build before closing D1.

non_blocking_findings.fix: Optional: a debug_assert!(!argv.is_empty()) or a typed (program, args) param shape. Not blocking.

reviews.summary: NEEDS_REVISION (orchestrator deep review + 2 adversarial verifiers + Lima ground-truth run). The PRODUCTION code is high-quality and design-faithful: D-MTLS-14 signatures verbatim, InterceptError matching the pinned surface field-for-field, InterceptedConnection/Routed constructed-not-extended (expected_peer None per v1/#178), the 4-method MtlsEnforcement contract untouched (D7); the const-hoist blocker correctly surfaced and resolved as a clean single-cut migration (D8); careful cleanup-before-apply RAII and a prerouting chain that byte-matches the proven harness while faithfully omitting the test-only GAP-3 (D9); AC1/AC4 genuinely green under the gate. TWO BLOCKING defects. D1 (test-integrity / gate-not-green): the committed inbound AT FAILS DETERMINISTICALLY under the exact gate command roadmap AC5 names -- ground-truth on a verified-clean kernel (fwmark=0/nft=0/route=0) panics at :308 'got 3, expected 1', reproduced 3x; the failing preclean-hygiene assertion was added in the LATER mutation-closure commit 93ca1eab (13:50Z) AFTER the logged GREEN/COMMIT phases (13:22-13:26Z) and the gated suite was never re-run green, so 06-02's COMMIT EXECUTED PASS is contradicted by the committed state -- AND the failure aborts before AC3, so the gate gives ZERO executed proof of the step's primary deliverable (inbound orig-dst recovery). RECOMMENDATION: (1) fix the preclean so the test passes, RE-RUN the exact gate command to green on a clean kernel, and confirm AC3 (orig-dst) executes -- do NOT re-log COMMIT EXECUTED PASS until then; (2) resolve/scope D2 before 06-03 wires per-alloc inbound (per-virt table keying within the pinned signature, OR a tracked HAZARD marker + documented single-intercept v1 scope -- surface to user/architect); (3) add the F5 exemption assertion (D3); (4) re-run the diff-scoped mutation gate after the suite is green (D4).
